metadata_information.title: Optimal pre-merger notification mechanisms - incentives and efficiency of mandatory and voluntary schemes
metadata_information.version: Python script, from D&R API, generated on 2026-03-19

title_statement.title: Optimal pre-merger notification mechanisms - incentives and efficiency of mandatory and voluntary schemes

document_description.abstract: The authors compare the two merger control systems currently employed worldwide: a mandatory system based on merger size threshold and a voluntary system with ex-post monitoring and fines. The voluntary system possesses two informational advantages: (i) the enforcement agency employs more information -verifiable and non verifiable parameters- to decide the set of mergers to investigate, and (ii) the first move of merging firms reveals useful information to the agency about the competitive risk of a merger. If fines for undue omission to notify are upward limited, then a mixed mechanism is optimal, where small transactions are under a voluntary regime while the big mergers are obliged to report. Remedies for fixing anticompetitive mergers act as an instrument that induces firms to notify the operation, improving further the advantage of the voluntary mechanism.
